title: FINRA Case 25-02453 — Kelechi Ukazim v. Robinhood Financial, LLC., Robinhood Securities, LLC
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-02453
Kelechi Ukazim
vs.
Respondents Hearing Site: Houston, Texas
Robinhood Financial LLC and
Robinhood Securities, LLC
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Customer vs. Members
This matter proceeded pursuant to Rule 12800 of the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”).
The customer did not request a hearing.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
Claimant Kelechi Ukazim (“Claimant”) appeared pro se.
For Respondents Robinhood Financial LLC and Robinhood Securities, LLC, collectively,
“Respondents”: Simeon M. Schopf, Esq., Robinhood, Washington, District of Columbia.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: November 7, 2025.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: November 7, 2025.
Statement of Answer and Counterclaim filed by Respondents on or about: December 29, 2025.
Respondents signed the Submission Agreement: December 29, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted the following causes of action: unauthorized
trading/negligence; breach of contract; breach of fiduciary duty/duty to act in customer’s best
interest; and violations of securities laws and FINRA rules. The causes of action related to
Claimant’s allegation that Respondent was liable for losses resulting from unauthorized trades in
his brokerage account, specifically Respondents’ October 2025 liquidation of approximately
4,894 shares of Lithium Americas Corp (LAC) stock.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-02453
Award Page 2 of 4
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer and Counterclaim, Respondents denied
the allegations made in the Statement of Claim and asserted various affirmative defenses. In the
Counterclaim, Respondents asserted the following cause of action: breach of contract. The cause
of action related to Respondents’ allegation that pursuant to the terms of the Customer
Agreement, Claimant agreed that he was liable for any debit balance or deficiency in his
account. Respondents further alleged that following the liquidation of Claimant’s account
positions as a result of the reversed deposits, Claimant owed a debit balance of $5,814.48,
which Claimant has failed to pay Respondents.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant requested compensatory damages in the amount of
$10,425.00; pre- and post-judgment interest, arbitration costs, and other fees and expenses;
and punitive and/or exemplary damages.
In the Statement of Answer and Counterclaim, Respondents requested that the claims asserted
in the Statement of Claim be denied; an award in their favor and against Claimant in the amount
of $5,814.48; that all costs and fees be assessed to Claimant; and any further relief as
determined by the Arbitrator.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrator acknowledges having read the pleadings and other materials filed by the parties.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the Arbitrator has decided and determined in full and final
resolution of the issues submitted for determination as follows:
1. Claimant’s claims are denied in their entirety.
2. Claimant is liable for and shall pay to Respondents the sum of $5,814.48 in compensatory
damages.
3. FINRA Dispute Resolution Services shall retain the $425.00 filing fee that Claimant deposited
previously.
4. FINRA Dispute Resolution Services shall retain the $750.00 filing fee that Respondents
deposited previously.
5. Claimant is liable for and shall pay to Respondents the sum of $750.00 to reimburse
Respondents for the filing fee previously paid to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services.
6. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein, including any requests for
punitive damages, treble damages, and attorneys’ fees, are denied.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-02453
Award Page 3 of 4
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, Respondents have paid to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services the
$495.00 Member Surcharge previously invoiced.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-02453
Award Page 4 of 4
ARBITRATOR
Heather Gaile Layton - Sole Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Arbitrator's Signature
Heather Gaile Layton 03/27/2026
Heather Gaile Layton Signature Date
Sole Public Arbitrator
